privacy.title: Privacy Policy
privacy.intro: Neetpix is committed to protecting your privacy. Most of our tools process your files entirely in your browser — your data never touches our servers.
privacy.localProcessingDesc: The following tools process all data entirely in your browser. Files you upload are never sent to any server:
privacy.localProcessingTools: Remove Background, Image to PDF, PDF to Word, Image Compress
privacy.apiProcessingDesc: The Screenshot Translate tool runs OCR locally in your browser, but sends the recognized text to our translation API proxy for translation. The text is not stored on our servers and is discarded immediately after translation.
privacy.dataRetentionDesc: We do not store your files or translated text. All processing is ephemeral. We only collect anonymous usage analytics (page views, tool usage counts) via Vercel Analytics, which does not use cookies or track individual users.
privacy.thirdPartyDesc: We use the following third-party services: Vercel (hosting and analytics), MyMemory Translation API (for screenshot translation). These services have their own privacy policies.
privacy.contactDesc: If you have questions about this privacy policy, please contact us at privacy@neetpix.com

about.title: About Neetpix
about.description: Neetpix is a free online tool platform built on a privacy-first principle. All processing happens in your browser - your files never leave your device.
about.intro: Neetpix was born from a simple frustration: most online tool sites are bloated with ads, require signups, upload your files to unknown servers, or lock core features behind paywalls. We wanted to build something different - a clean, fast, and genuinely free tool platform that respects your privacy.
about.privacyDesc: Every tool on Neetpix processes your files directly in your browser using WebAssembly and modern web APIs. Your files are never uploaded to any server. This isn't just a marketing claim - it's a technical architecture decision.
about.toolsPdf: 12 PDF tools - convert, merge, split, compress, watermark, encrypt, crop, rotate and more
about.toolsImage: 10 image tools - format convert, EXIF strip, resize/crop, grid split, ID photo, OCR, blur, remove background, compress, watermark
about.toolsGenerator: 2 generator tools - QR code and chart generator, fully local
about.toolsTranslate: 1 translation tool - screenshot OCR translation with auto-detect
about.toolsTotal: 25 tools in total — all free, no signup, no upload

faq.title: FAQ
faq.removeBackground1a: Yes, completely free. AI runs in your browser, so we have no server costs.
faq.removeBackground2a: JPG, PNG, and WebP up to 20MB.
faq.removeBackground3: Does the free version have a watermark?
faq.removeBackground3a: Yes, the free version includes a small Neetpix watermark. Future Pro version will offer watermark-free downloads.
faq.imageToPdf1: Can I reorder images after uploading?
faq.imageToPdf1a: Yes, drag and drop any thumbnail to reorder.
faq.imageToPdf2a: A4, Letter, or fit to original image size. Portrait or landscape orientation.
faq.imageToPdf3: Is there a limit on the number of images?
faq.imageToPdf3a: No hard limit, but we recommend under 50 images for best performance.
faq.pdfToWord1: Does PDF to Word preserve formatting?
faq.pdfToWord1a: This tool extracts text content. Complex layouts, images, and tables may not be fully preserved.
faq.pdfToWord2a: 50MB. Processing happens in your browser, so very large files may be slow.
faq.pdfToWord3a: Scanned PDFs without a text layer cannot be extracted. The tool requires selectable text in the PDF.
faq.wordToPdf1: Is the Word to PDF converter free?
faq.wordToPdf1a: Yes, completely free with no limits on file count or size (up to 50MB per file). No signup, no watermark, no hidden fees.
faq.wordToPdf2: Are my documents uploaded to a server?
faq.wordToPdf2a: No. All processing happens in your browser. Your documents never leave your device, ensuring complete privacy.
faq.wordToPdf3a: We preserve text content with basic formatting including headings, paragraphs, and lists. Complex layouts, images, and tables may be simplified.
faq.screenshotTranslate1a: English and Chinese, in both directions (EN→ZH and ZH→EN), plus auto-detect.
faq.screenshotTranslate2: Can I paste a screenshot directly?
faq.screenshotTranslate2a: Yes, press Ctrl+V (or Cmd+V on Mac) to paste a screenshot from your clipboard.
faq.screenshotTranslate3a: OCR accuracy depends on image clarity. Translation uses the MyMemory API. For best results, use clear, high-contrast screenshots.
faq.imageCompress1a: You control the quality level (1-100%). Lower quality means smaller file size but more visible compression artifacts.
faq.imageCompress2: Can I convert formats during compression?
faq.imageCompress2a: Yes, you can output as JPG, PNG, or WebP regardless of the original format.
faq.imageCompress3: Is there a batch download option?
faq.imageCompress3a: Yes, upload multiple images and click Download All to get a ZIP file.
faq.pdfMerge1a: Yes, completely free. All merging happens in your browser with no server costs.
faq.pdfMerge2: Can I reorder PDF files before merging?
faq.pdfMerge2a: Yes, use the up/down arrows next to each file to change the order.
faq.pdfMerge3: How many PDFs can I merge?
faq.pdfMerge3a: There's no hard limit, but we recommend under 20 files for best performance.
faq.pdfSplit1: What page range formats are supported?
faq.pdfSplit1a: Use formats like 1-3,5,7-9. Single pages and ranges separated by commas are both supported.
faq.pdfSplit2: Can I split into multiple files at once?
faq.pdfSplit2a: Yes, enter multiple ranges separated by commas. Each range generates a separate PDF file.
faq.pdfSplit3a: Password-protected PDFs cannot be split. Please remove the password first.
faq.pdfCompress1a: This tool uses lossless structural compression. It re-serializes the PDF to reduce size without degrading content.
faq.pdfCompress2: How much can I reduce the file size?
faq.pdfCompress2a: Results vary. Already-optimized PDFs may see little reduction, while verbose PDFs can shrink significantly.
faq.pdfCompress3: What's the difference between Light and Strong mode?
faq.pdfCompress3a: Light mode uses lossless structural compression (best for text PDFs, 5-20% smaller). Strong mode rasterizes each page as a compressed JPEG image (best for scanned/image PDFs, up to 80% smaller, but text becomes non-searchable).
faq.pdfWatermark1: Can I customize the watermark text?
faq.pdfWatermark1a: Yes, enter any text. The default is CONFIDENTIAL.
faq.pdfWatermark2a: Center places one watermark in the middle, while Tiled repeats it across the whole page.
faq.pdfWatermark3a: Yes. We render watermark text via Canvas, which supports Chinese, English, and any language your browser supports.
faq.pdfPageNumbers1: Can I choose where page numbers appear?
faq.pdfPageNumbers1a: Yes, choose bottom center, bottom left, or bottom right.
faq.pdfPageNumbers2: Can I start from a different number?
faq.pdfPageNumbers2a: Yes, set any start number. The first page will use that number.
faq.pdfPageNumbers3a: Plain number (1), Page X, or Page X / Y. All use the Helvetica font.
faq.imageWatermark1a: Completely free. All processing happens in your browser, no server costs.
faq.imageWatermark2: Does it support Chinese watermark text?
faq.imageWatermark2a: Yes. We use a system font stack including PingFang SC and Microsoft YaHei, so Chinese renders cleanly without garbled characters.
faq.imageWatermark3: Are my images uploaded to a server?
faq.imageWatermark3a: No. All images are processed locally in your browser and never leave your device—ideal for sensitive documents like ID cards.
faq.pdfEncrypt1a: Yes. We use AES-256 encryption, the industry standard for PDF protection. All processing happens in your browser.
faq.pdfEncrypt2: Can I recover a forgotten password?
faq.pdfEncrypt2a: No. PDF encryption is one-way. If you forget the password, the PDF cannot be recovered. Please store your password safely.
faq.pdfEncrypt3: Is there a file size limit?
faq.pdfEncrypt3a: Yes, 50MB per file. Processing happens in your browser, so very large files may be slow.
faq.pdfDecrypt1: Can I decrypt a PDF without knowing the password?
faq.pdfDecrypt1a: No. You must know the password to remove it. This tool is for removing passwords from PDFs you own, not for cracking.
faq.pdfDecrypt2a: Yes, as long as you own the PDF or have permission. This tool is for removing passwords from your own documents.
faq.pdfDecrypt3: What if I enter the wrong password?
faq.pdfDecrypt3a: You'll see an error message. Simply re-enter the correct password and try again.
faq.pdfCrop1a: You enter a percentage (0-100) for each side — top, right, bottom, left. That portion of the page is trimmed from that edge. Top+Bottom and Left+Right must each total less than 100%.
faq.pdfCrop2: Will cropping reduce the file size?
faq.pdfCrop2a: Usually not significantly. Cropping only changes the visible page area; the underlying content stream is preserved, so file size stays roughly the same.
faq.pdfCrop3a: No. Password-protected PDFs cannot be cropped. Please remove the password first using the PDF Decrypt tool.
faq.pdfRotate1: Can I rotate only specific pages?
faq.pdfRotate1a: Yes. Choose "Specific pages" and enter page ranges like 1-3,5,7-9. The other pages keep their original orientation.
faq.pdfRotate2a: 90° (clockwise), 180°, and 270° (counter-clockwise). Pick the angle that fits your document.
faq.pdfRotate3a: No. Rotation only updates the page's orientation metadata, so the content quality is fully preserved.
faq.imageConvert1: Is HEIC to JPG conversion really free?
faq.imageConvert1a: Yes, completely free with no limits. All conversion happens in your browser, so we have no server costs and your photos stay private.
faq.imageConvert2: Can I convert multiple images at once?
faq.imageConvert2a: Yes, upload multiple images and we'll convert them all in one go. Download each file individually or grab them as a ZIP.
faq.imageConvert3a: JPG, PNG, WebP, AVIF, and HEIC. Convert in any direction — for example, HEIC from an iPhone to JPG for broader compatibility.
faq.imageExif1: What is EXIF metadata and why should I care?
faq.imageExif1a: EXIF data includes GPS location, camera info, and timestamps embedded by your camera or phone. Stripping it protects your privacy before sharing photos online.
faq.imageExif2: Does stripping EXIF affect image quality?
faq.imageExif2a: No, stripping only removes metadata. The image pixels remain identical, so quality and resolution are fully preserved.
faq.imageExif3: Are my photos uploaded to a server?
faq.imageExif3a: No. All EXIF processing happens locally in your browser, so your photos never leave your device — ideal for protecting GPS data in personal photos.
faq.imageResize1: What social media presets are available?
faq.imageResize1a: Instagram Square (1:1), Instagram Story (9:16), Twitter Post (16:9), and more. Pick a preset and we'll resize to the exact dimensions automatically.
faq.imageResize2: Can I resize multiple images at once?
faq.imageResize2a: Yes, resize mode supports batch processing. Upload multiple images, apply the same dimensions, and download them all as a ZIP file.
faq.imageResize3a: Resizing changes pixel dimensions, which may soften detail when upscaling. We use high-quality Lanczos resampling to minimize loss, and downscaling preserves detail well.
faq.imageIdPhoto1: Is the ID photo generation really free?
faq.imageIdPhoto1a: Yes, completely free. All processing happens in your browser using AI models, so we have no server costs and your photos never leave your device.
faq.imageIdPhoto2: What photo should I upload for best results?
faq.imageIdPhoto2a: Use a photo with a plain background, good lighting, and face the camera directly. Avoid heavy shadows, sunglasses, or hats for the cleanest ID photo output.
faq.imageIdPhoto3: Are my photos uploaded to a server?
faq.imageIdPhoto3a: No. Your photos are processed entirely in your browser using AI models. Nothing is uploaded, so your portrait stays fully private.
faq.imageOcr1a: Chinese (Simplified), English, and Japanese. You can enable multiple languages at once for mixed-language images.
faq.imageOcr2a: Accuracy depends on image quality and text clarity. Clear, high-resolution images with good contrast give the best results, while blurry or skewed text may be less accurate.
faq.imageOcr3: Is my image uploaded for OCR processing?
faq.imageOcr3a: No, OCR runs entirely in your browser using Tesseract.js. Your images and the extracted text never leave your device.
faq.imageBlur1a: No, this tool uses manual selection for maximum privacy and reliability. You draw a box over each area you want to blur, giving you full control.
faq.imageBlur2a: Yes, you can blur multiple regions by selecting and applying blur one by one. Each blur is applied permanently to the image before the next.
faq.imageBlur3: What blur intensity should I use?
faq.imageBlur3a: Light for subtle blurring, Medium for most cases, and Heavy for complete obscuration of faces or license plates.
faq.imageGridSplit1: Do I need to upload my image to a server?
faq.imageGridSplit1a: No. All splitting happens in your browser. Your image never leaves your device, so it's safe for personal photos.
faq.imageGridSplit2a: Built-in presets for 2×2, 3×3, and 4×4. You can also choose Custom and set any rows × columns between 1 and 6.
faq.imageGridSplit3: Can I use it for an Instagram nine-grid post?
faq.imageGridSplit3a: Yes. Choose the 3×3 layout, split your image, and the 9 pieces are downloaded as a ZIP — ready to post in order.
faq.qrCode1: Do I need to upload anything or be online?
faq.qrCode1a: No. QR codes are generated entirely in your browser. No upload, no internet connection required after the page loads.
faq.qrCode2a: Plain text, URLs, email addresses, WiFi credentials (SSID + password + encryption), and vCard contact info (name, phone, email).
faq.qrCode3: Can I customize the QR code colors?
faq.qrCode3a: Yes. You can pick any foreground and background color, choose the size, and select the error correction level (L/M/Q/H).
faq.chartGenerator1: Do I need to upload my data?
faq.chartGenerator1a: No. All chart rendering happens locally in your browser. Your data never leaves your device.
faq.chartGenerator2a: Bar, line, and pie charts. You can add multiple series for bar and line charts (pie uses the first series only).
faq.chartGenerator3a: PNG (raster) and SVG (vector). SVG scales to any size without quality loss, ideal for print and presentations.

error.boundaryDesc: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.
